error_signature: self signed certificate in certificate chain
title: Self-signed certificate error when connecting to PostgreSQL
symptom: When attempting to connect to a PostgreSQL database with SSL, n8n throws 'self signed certificate in certificate chain'. This prevents the database connection from being established.
not_this_if: Using a valid SSL certificate from a trusted CA
root_cause: The error occurs because the PostgreSQL server is using a self-signed certificate that is not trusted by the client (n8n). This can happen when the CA certificate is not properly configured or when the SSL options are not set to accept self-signed certificates.
fix_steps: Ensure the CA certificate is correctly mapped in the Docker container.
Set the environment variable DB_POSTGRESDB_SSL_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED to false.
Restart the n8n instance to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Set DB_POSTGRESDB_SSL_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED to false and restart n8n.
safety_notes: Setting SSL_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED to false may expose you to security risks.
